source.title: Mosaic RAS/MAPK variants cause sporadic vascular malformations which respond to targeted therapy

initial_request.research_problem: Sporadic vascular malformations (VMs) have high morbidity and limited treatment options, and a substantial proportion of patients have no mutations in known associated genes.
initial_request.known_context: Therapeutic options are severely limited, and multidisciplinary management remains challenging, particularly for high-flow arteriovenous malformations (AVM).

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Sporadic VMs have high morbidity and limited treatment options, and many patients lack mutations in known genes.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Unknown genetic cause for a substantial proportion of sporadic VMs.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Mosaic RAS/MAPK pathway variants cause sporadic VMs.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Formulate hypothesis based on known role of RAS/MAPK pathway in other mosaic disorders.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Hypothesis formulated that mosaic-activating variants in RAS/MAPK pathway genes cause VM.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Design study to test hypothesis using ultradeep sequencing.

evidence.quote_or_span: Sporadic vascular malformations (VMs) are complex congenital anomalies of blood vessels that lead to stroke, life-threatening bleeds, disfigurement, overgrowth, and/or pain.
Therapeutic options are severely limited, and multidisciplinary management remains challenging, particularly for high-flow arteriovenous malformations (AVM).
a substantial proportion of patients have no mutations in known associated genes
Here, we discover that multiple mosaic-activating variants in 4 genes of the RAS/MAPK pathway cause VM and model therapy in an animal model.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Hypothesis that mosaic RAS/MAPK variants cause VMs.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to detect low-frequency mosaic variants in affected tissue.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Deep sequencing of affected tissue will identify mosaic RAS/MAPK variants.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Design study to sequence DNA from affected tissue of 160 children with VMs, excluding known genetic causes.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Study designed to include both high-flow and low-flow VMs.

evidence.quote_or_span: To investigate the pathogenesis of sporadic intracranial and extracranial VMs in 160 children in which known genetic causes had been excluded, we sequenced DNA from affected tissue and optimized analysis for detection of low mutant allele frequency.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Study designed to sequence affected tissue from 160 children.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Unknown whether mosaic RAS/MAPK variants are present in VMs.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Mosaic variants in RAS/MAPK genes will be identified in affected tissue.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform deep sequencing and variant calling.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Variants in KRAS, NRAS, BRAF, and MAP2K1 identified in 9 of 25 AVMs and 5 of 135 low-flow VMs, with allele frequencies 3-26%.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Validate variants by a second method and check absence in blood.

evidence.quote_or_span: Using deep next-generation sequencing of affected tissue, known or predicted pathogenic variants in RAS/MAPK pathway genes were identified in 9 of the 25 patients with AVMs
Identified variants were in KRAS (n = 4) and BRAF (n = 1)
a cluster of variants was identified in exon 2 of MAP2K1 (n = 4)
Using deep next-generation sequencing of affected tissue, known or predicted pathogenic variants in RAS/MAPK pathway genes were identified in 9 of the 25 patients with AVMs
Of the 135 patients with low-flow VMs, 5 had mosaic RAS gene variants, 4 in KRAS and 1 in NRAS

To investigate the pathogenesis of sporadic intracranial and extracranial VMs in 160 children in which known genetic causes had been excluded, we sequenced DNA from affected tissue and optimized analysis for detection of low mutant allele frequency.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Mosaic variants discovered in RAS/MAPK genes.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to confirm variants are somatic and not germline.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Variants are somatic mosaic and absent in blood.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Confirm variants by a second method and test paired blood samples.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Variants confirmed by a second method and absent in paired blood samples (n=4/9 AVMs, n=4/5 low-flow).
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Analyze structural impact of MAP2K1 variants.

evidence.quote_or_span: confirmed by a second method
confirmed by a second method

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Structural impact of MAP2K1 variants unknown.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: MAP2K1 exon 2 variants destabilize inactive kinase structure.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform structural analysis of MAP2K1 variants.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Variants affect helix A (residues 44-58) and are predicted to destabilize inactive conformation.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Test functional impact of variants on ERK phosphorylation.

evidence.quote_or_span: All were found to affect helix A in the 3D protein structure (residues 44–58).
these variants are therefore predicted to destabilize the conformation of the inactive state.
Somatic variants in MAP2K1 cluster in exon 2 and are predicted to destabilize the 3D structure of the inactive form of the kinase.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: MAP2K1 and BRAF variants identified and structurally analyzed.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Functional impact of variants on MAPK pathway activity unknown.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Mutant MAP2K1 and BRAF increase ERK phosphorylation.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Transiently overexpress mutants in HEK293T cells and measure ERK phosphorylation.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Mutant BRAF V600E, MAP2K1 K57N, and MAP2K1 Q58_E62del increased ERK phosphorylation vs WT and mock.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Test impact on vascular tube formation in HUVECs.

evidence.quote_or_span: Transient overexpression of MAP2K1 and BRAF mutants in HEK293T cells led to increased phosphorylation of ERK compared to either overexpression of corresponding WT genes or to mock-transfected controls at 24 hours

HEK293T cells (ATCC, catalog CRL-11268) were maintained per established protocols and were transfected with mutant and WT cDNA expression plasmids and an empty vector control using Lipofectamine 2000.

HEK293T cells (ATCC, catalog CRL-11268) were maintained per established protocols and were transfected with mutant and WT cDNA expression plasmids and an empty vector control using Lipofectamine 2000.

(A and B) Expression of mutant BRAF $^{V600E}$ , MAP2K1 $^{K57N}$ , and MAP2K1 $^{Q58\_E62del}$ in HEK293T cells leads to significantly increased phosphorylation of ERK detected by immunoblotting (representative blot shown from duplicate biological replicates), compared with WT gene overexpression and controls.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Mutants increase ERK phosphorylation in HEK293T cells.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Impact of mutants on endothelial cell function unknown.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Mutant overexpression disrupts vascular tube formation in HUVECs.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Overexpress mutants in HUVECs and assess tube formation on Geltrex.
evolution_trajectory.observation: BRAF V600E and MAP2K1 K57N disrupted tube formation, reducing master junctions, tube length, and mesh area.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Model variants in zebrafish to recapitulate VM phenotype.

evidence.quote_or_span: Overexpression of 2 of the same mutants in HUVEC led to disruption and disordering of spontaneous vascular tube formation between 6 and 24 hours compared with mock-transfected controls
significant reductions in mean number of master junctions, total vascular tube length, and total mesh area
HUVECs (Thermo Fisher, catalog number C0035C) were incubated in transfection reagents with a 1:3 DNA/Lipofectamine LTX ratio at 37°C, 5% (v/v) CO₂ for 48 hours.
(C) Expression of mutant BRAF $^{V600E}$ and MAP2K1 $^{K57N}$ in HUVECs seeded onto Geltrex Matrix leads to visible disruption of endothelial vascular tube formation compared with controls.

(A and B) Expression of mutant BRAF $^{V600E}$ , MAP2K1 $^{K57N}$ , and MAP2K1 $^{Q58\_E62del}$ in HEK293T cells leads to significantly increased phosphorylation of ERK detected by immunoblotting (representative blot shown from duplicate biological replicates), compared with WT gene overexpression and controls.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Mutants disrupt endothelial tube formation in vitro.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: In vivo relevance of variants unknown.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Mosaic expression of BRAF V600E or MAP2K1 Q58del in zebrafish endothelium causes VM-like lesions.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Generate transgenic zebrafish with endothelial-specific mosaic expression of mutants.
evolution_trajectory.observation: BRAF V600E caused disordered vessel formation and impeded blood flow in 15% of fish vs 0% WT; MAP2K1 Q58del also induced VM phenotype.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Test targeted therapy with BRAF inhibitor vemurafenib.

evidence.quote_or_span: In vitro characterization and 2 transgenic zebrafish AVM models that recapitulated the human phenotype validated the pathogenesis of the mutant alleles.
(C) Images of zebrafish expressing WT or mutant BRAF $^{V600E}$ in a mosaic fashion from a fli1a promoter in endothelial cells.
(D) Quantification of VM phenotype in BRAF $^{WT}$ -expressing (n = 511) and BRAF $^{V600E}$ -expressing (n = 779) zebrafish.

Notably, BRAF $^{V600E}$ , but not expression of WT BRAF, led to disordered vessel formation, leading to severely impeded blood flow, and recapitulated the clinical features of patient VMs (Figure 5, C–E, and Supplemental Video 1).

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Whether targeted therapy can rescue VM phenotype in vivo.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: BRAF inhibitor vemurafenib will restore blood flow in BRAF V600E zebrafish.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Treat BRAF V600E zebrafish with vemurafenib and assess blood flow.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Almost all vemurafenib-treated zebrafish exhibited improved blood flow compared to DMSO controls.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Analyze variant frequency differences between high- and low-flow VMs.

evidence.quote_or_span: treatment of AVM-BRAF mutant zebrafish with the BRAF inhibitor vemurafinib restored blood flow in AVM
almost all vemurafenib-treated zebrafish exhibited improved blood flow
VM BRAF $^{V600E}$ zebrafish were randomized prior to DMSO (n = 31) or vemurafenib (n = 19) treatment and blind scored.
(F) Schematic of VM treatment protocol and quantification of the percentage of VM BRAF $^{V600E}$ zebrafish with improved blood flow following treatment with vemurafenib.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Variants discovered in both high-flow and low-flow VMs.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Frequency difference between high-flow and low-flow VMs unexplained.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Variants are more frequent in high-flow VMs.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Variants were more frequent in high-flow than low-flow VMs.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Note limitation and potential for targeted therapy.

evidence.quote_or_span: These variants were more frequent in high-flow than low-flow VMs.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Frequency difference between high- and low-flow VMs observed.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Possible loss of mutations in fibroblast culture for low-flow cohort.
evolution_trajectory.observation: The cause of the difference in frequency of these variants between the high- and low-flow cohorts is not clear. As a minority of the low-flow cohort skin biopsies were cultured for fibroblasts before DNA extraction, it is possible that the mutations were either lost in culture or not present in fibroblasts.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Assess broader significance of findings for AVM management.

evidence.quote_or_span: The cause of the difference in frequency of these variants between the high- and low-flow cohorts is not clear. As a minority of the low-flow cohort skin biopsies were cultured for fibroblasts before DNA extraction, it is possible that the mutations were either lost in culture or not present in fibroblasts.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Mosaic mutations identified in a subset of AVMs.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Broader significance of findings for AVM management.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Mosaic mutations in a third of AVM cohort suggest potential for targeted medical therapy.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Assess proportion of AVM cohort with mosaic mutations.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Mosaic mutations found in a third of AVM cohort, including intracranial AVMs, suggesting targeted therapy potential.

evidence.quote_or_span: mosaic mutations in a third of our AVM cohort, including intracranial AVMs, will radically alter the concept of management of these VMs by introducing the possibility of targeted medical therapy.

validation_methods: in vitro functional assays (ERK phosphorylation, tube formation)
in vivo zebrafish model recapitulating phenotype
metrics: tube formation parameters (master junctions, tube length, mesh area)
success_verification.final_conclusion: Mosaic RAS/MAPK variants cause sporadic VMs, and targeted therapy with vemurafenib rescues the phenotype in a zebrafish model, suggesting potential for medical therapy in patients.
limitations: Unexplained difference in variant frequency between high- and low-flow cohorts; possible loss in fibroblast culture.
